newsSince: Bloom Energy rallied to record highs (+~40% over six sessions; $93B cap, +232% YTD), driven by a U.S. FERC directive to grid operators, Bloom's mid-year AI Data Center Power Report, and the Oracle agreement to deploy up to 2.8GW of Bloom fuel cells (1.2GW already in deployment). Q1 2026 beat with $751M revenue and $70.7M net income. Thesis is genuinely strengthening on AI-power demand, but the name has fully rerated and remains a crowded benchmark on the residual-alpha page.

sources.label: Bloom Energy $5B AI deal + Wyoming offtake (Yahoo Finance)
Bloom Energy AI power deals pile up (StocksToTrade)
Bloom near record on AI power demand (InvestorIdeas)
Bloom Energy near record on AI power demand
Bloom Energy record near AI power demand - InvestorIdeas

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha-vs-passives lens, the Brookfield/Oracle news is real but only confirms the crowded/rerated-benchmark bucket: +232% YTD, ~$93B cap, score already 0 at rank 100. No untapped residual alpha; hold the demoted floor.
verdicts.bull: Bankable SOFC onsite power is the fastest grid-bottleneck workaround; multi-GW Oracle/AEP/Brookfield backlog.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis right, price ahead of it: ~$93B cap fully discounts the AI-power option already.
verdicts.bear: +232% YTD leaves no residual alpha vs passives; any AI-capex wobble or gas-policy shift hits hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Backlog conversion and 2GW capacity ramp execution.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/valuation; dependence on continued hyperscaler power scramble.
